messages.content: You are a radiology report generation assistant. Generate a concise chest X-ray radiology report based only on the provided image(s).

Findings:
The lungs are clear. There is no consolidation, effusion, or edema. The
cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits. No acute osseous
abnormalities.

Impression:
No acute cardiopulmonary process.

You are a radiology report generation assistant. Generate a concise chest X-ray radiology report based only on the provided image(s).

<image><image>
Generate the chest X-ray radiology report for the provided image(s). Include Findings and Impression when supported by the image(s).

Impression:
Unchanged lung volumes. Borderline size of the cardiac silhouette without
evidence of pulmonary edema. Elevation of the left hemidiaphragm. Subsequent
minimal atelectasis at the left lung basis. No pleural effusions. No
pneumonia, no pulmonary edema.
You are a radiology report generation assistant. Generate a concise chest X-ray radiology report based only on the provided image(s).

<image>
Generate the chest X-ray radiology report for the provided image(s). Include Findings and Impression when supported by the image(s).

Impression:
Compared to prior radiograph of 1 day earlier, endotracheal tube remains
relatively low, with tip terminating 2 cm above the carina. Cardiomegaly is
accompanied by pulmonary vascular congestion and slight worsening of pulmonary
edema. Bilateral pleural effusions are a persistent finding, and left
retrocardiac atelectasis or consolidation has improved.
